blocks:
Ayahs

أَلَمْ تَرَ كَيْفَ فَعَلَ رَبُّكَ بِأَصْحَـٰبِ ٱلْفِيلِ (۱)
Have you not regarded how your Lord dealt with the army of the elephants? (1)
أَلَمْ يَجْعَلْ كَيْدَهُمْ فِى تَضْلِيلٍ (۲)
Did He not make their stratagems go awry, (2)
وَأَرْسَلَ عَلَيْهِمْ طَيْرًا أَبَابِيلَ (۳)
and send against them flocks of birds (3)
تَرْمِيهِم بِحِجَارَةٍ مِّن سِجِّيلٍ (٤)
pelting them with stones of shale, (4)
فَجَعَلَهُمْ كَعَصْفٍ مَّأْكُولٍۭ (٥)
thus making them like chewed-up straw? (5)

Key Points

Contrary to what is commonly known among people, "Ababil" (ابابیل) is not the name of a specific bird, but rather means "in flocks" and "group after group." "Ṭayran Abābīl" (طیراً ابابیل) means birds descending upon them, flock after flock, in scattered groups. The word "ṭayran" (طیراً) refers to the genus of birds, not a single bird.

Regarding the context of the revelation of this Ayah, we read: A king named Abrahah built a temple of marble in Yemen and commanded people to visit and circumambulate it. An Arab man showed disrespect to this temple. Abrahah brought an army mounted on elephants near Mecca to destroy the Ka'bah in retaliation for that disrespect. Allah then sent birds toward them, carrying small stones in their beaks. With the showering of the small stones, Abrahah's army fell to the ground like chewed straw and were destroyed. That year was named the Year of the Elephant (ʿĀm al-Fīl), and the Prophet of Islam was also born in that same year. (169)

The People of the Elephant were both crushed and humiliated. "ʿAṣf Ma'kūl" (عصف مأكول), which is likened to straw that has been eaten by an animal—meaning dung—is the worst form of humiliation.

(169) In the year 1358 Solar Hijri (1979 CE), following the victory of the Islamic Revolution in Iran and the occupation of the US Embassy by Muslim students, America sent a number of airplanes and helicopters toward Iran to free its hostages and overthrow the Islamic regime. However, by the Will of Allah, the sands of the Tabas desert created such a storm that some of the airplanes were grounded and their pilots burned, while others fled and left the area. He destroyed them.

Messages

1- To warn criminals, use examples that are immediate and well-known. (The fame of the People of the Elephant was so great that it was as if it happened right before their eyes and needed no proof.) «اَلَم تَر»

2- Do not forget history. «الم تر»

3- The Ka'bah was sacred even before Islam, and violating the sanctity of holy sites incurs punishment. «كیف فعل ربّك باصحاب الفیل»

4- Divine wrath against criminals is a manifestation of Allah's Lordship (Rubūbiyyah). «فعل ربّك»

5- Birds are Allah's agents and possess awareness. «ارسل علیهم طیراً ابابیل»

6- Being aware of the enemy's plot and frustrating it is important. «یجعل كیدهم فى تضلیل»

7- The enemy uses the largest military equipment (such as elephants) for attack, but Allah destroys them using the smallest creatures, namely the birds of the sky. «اصحاب الفیل... طیراً ابابیل»

8- The enemies of Allah are powerless, no matter how well-equipped they may be. «اصحاب الفیل... كعصف مأكول»

9- The severest punishment is reserved for insulting holy sites. (Other nations that were destroyed were not turned into chewed straw; rather, they either fell upon the ground or were drowned in water.) «فجعلهم كعصف مأكول»
